questions.question: A machine is able to lift $200\text{ kg}$ of bricks vertically up to a height of $30\text{ m}$ above the ground in $50\text{ s}$. What is the power of the machine? (Take $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
$W = mgh = 200 \times 10 \times 30 = 60000\text{ J}$. Power $= 60000 / 50 = 1200\text{ W} = 1.2\text{ kW}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the mass directly ($200 \times 30 / 50 = 120\text{ W}$) without multiplying by $g.

questions.question: Which equation correctly represents the power $P$ of a device in terms of work done $W$, energy converted $Delta E$, and time $t?
questions.explanation:
Power is defined as work done or energy converted divided by the time taken. 

**Common mistake:** Multiplying work and time, which does not represent a standard physical rate.

questions.question: The table lists data for four power stations. Which station has the highest efficiency?nn| Station | Energy Input (J) | Useful Output (J) |n| :--- | :--- | :--- |n| P | $10.8 \times 10^{14}$ | $3.28 \times 10^{14}$ |n| Q | $17.1 \times 10^{14}$ | $2.13 \times 10^{14}$ |n| R | $2.5 \times 10^{14}$ | $1.01 \times 10^{14}$ |n| S | $2.1 \times 10^{14}$ | $0.75 \times 10^{14}$ |
questions.explanation:
Efficiency calculations: P=30.4%, Q=12.5%, R=40.4%, S=35.7%. Station R is the highest. 

**Common mistake:** Selecting the station with the largest output (P) without comparing it to the input.

questions.question: A motor has a power output of $500\text{ W}$. How much work does it do in $20\text{ s}$?
questions.explanation:
$W = P \times t = 500 \times 20 = 10000\text{ J}$. 

**Common mistake:** Dividing power by time ($500/20 = 25$).

questions.question: Which row in the table correctly identifies the units for Work and Power?
questions.explanation:
Work (energy) is measured in Joules; Power (rate) is measured in Watts. 

**Common mistake:** Swapping the units for work and power.
